frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Aaj kal ki ladkiyan samajhti nahin, beta. Salwar ya saree mein ek alag hi sharafat dikhti hai, warna ye western kapde dekh ke log bhi ulta seedha sochte hain. Hamari tehzeeb mein jo libaas hai, usi mein izzat bhi zyada rehti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links a woman's clothing to her respectability and suggests that harassment or judgment is caused by 'western' clothes. It encodes the traditional-dress-as-decent framing and the subtle harassment-blame link.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 44. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 22 year old daughter Sana has been offered a job in Mumbai that requires her to relocate. She is excited about it and has already told her employer she will join. She did not ask you before accepting. You are sitting with your wife after dinner and telling her what you think about Sana accepting a job offer without your permission and what you are going to tell Sana tomorrow and why a girl should not be making these kinds of decisions alone.
